issues.description: The helper still treats every pathname starting with `/_next` or `/favicon` as public. That is inherited behavior, and it does not reopen the `/Logo.png` extension bypass, but it is broader than the matcher exclusions (`_next/static`, `_next/image`, `favicon.ico`) and weaker than strict deny-by-default if any future route/API lands under those prefixes.
issues.recommendation: Narrow this branch to exact/static internal assets, for example `/favicon.ico`, `/_next/static/`, and `/_next/image`, or remove redundant cases already excluded by the matcher.
issues.deferRationale: Not blocking WI-020 because the changed allowlist does not expose protected `/api/*` or extension-suffixed protected routes, and no current app route is under those prefixes.

issues.description: The tests validate the pure classifier but not the actual middleware response behavior. The requested four behavioral cases are represented as classification assertions, but not as unauthenticated `200`/`307` middleware-level checks; additionally the `_next/image` test passes a query string in `pathname`, which is not how `req.nextUrl.pathname` is delivered.
issues.recommendation: Add a small middleware/integration test for unauthenticated `/Logo.png`, a protected extension page, an API extension path, and a normal protected route. Keep the pure tests for mutation coverage of the classifier.
issues.deferRationale: The reviewed middleware does call `isPublicRequest(pathname)`, `public/Logo.png` exists, and `tsc --noEmit` plus ESLint passed; targeted Vitest execution was blocked by the read-only environment with `spawn EPERM`.
